text: The SRS shall address the securing of data artifacts.
The SRS shall address the processes supporting the creation, retrieval, updating, and deletion of information objects.
The Business Architecture shall include a list of use cases and integration requirements.
The Business Architecture shall define business services at the functional level.
The Application Architecture shall model applications as logical components.
The Application Architecture shall define the services each logical component provides or consumes.
The Data Architecture shall model EIM data consistently across all integration services to ensure semantic interoperability.
The Technology Architecture shall define how services interact to support end-to-end EIM business processes.
The EIM Competency Center shall provide governance and knowledge distribution.
The system shall provide the ability to share models with external entities.
The system shall support Business to Business (B2B) model sharing.
The system shall support Consumer (B2C) model sharing.
The system shall support Smart Grid data management requirements.
The system shall support non-Smart Grid data management requirements.
The system shall include self-healing and self-discovery capabilities.
The Data Architecture shall include persistent data store architectural requirements.
The system shall support collocated Smart Grid and non-Smart Grid data within persistent data stores.
The system shall utilize localized data stores.
The system shall utilize centralized data stores.
The EIM shall incorporate the IEC Common Information Model (CIM).
The EIM shall enable IEC Common Information Model-based messaging.
The EIM shall enable the creation of persistent data stores based on the IEC Common Information Model.
The information model shall define common definitions of data concepts.
The information model shall allow aliases of data concepts.
The Technical Architecture shall include a metadata repository.
